name: zero_size_alignment
code:
proof fn phi_1_zero_size_alignment(addr: nat)
    requires
        addr == 0,
    ensures
        aligned(addr, 0),
{
}
reason: Alignment with size 0 should be undefined/invalid, not trivially true via 0 % 0 == 0 in SMT

name: nonzero_zero_size_alignment
code:
proof fn phi_2_nonzero_zero_size_alignment(addr: nat)
    requires
        addr > 0,
    ensures
        aligned(addr, 0),
{
}
reason: Any nonzero address being aligned to size 0 would indicate the spec fails to guard against zero divisor

name: upward_alignment_implication
code:
proof fn phi_3_upward_alignment_implication(addr: nat)
    requires
        aligned(addr, 4),
    ensures
        aligned(addr, 8),
{
}
reason: 4-alignment does not imply 8-alignment (e.g. addr=4), so this being provable would mean the spec is too weak

name: off_by_one_aligned
code:
proof fn phi_4_off_by_one_aligned(addr: nat, size: nat)
    requires
        aligned(addr, size),
        size > 1,
    ensures
        aligned((addr + 1) as nat, size),
{
}
reason: Shifting an aligned address by 1 must break alignment for any size > 1; provability implies the spec is vacuous

name: product_sum_alignment
code:
proof fn phi_5_product_sum_alignment(addr: nat, s1: nat, s2: nat)
    requires
        s1 > 1,
        s2 > 1,
        aligned(addr, s1 * s2),
    ensures
        aligned(addr, s1 + s2),
{
}
reason: Divisibility by a product does not imply divisibility by the sum (e.g. 6 % 6 == 0 but 6 % 5 != 0)
